UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS DAVID LEVY, ELEANOR LEVY, AND LEVY FAMILY TRUST'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS DENIED. MOVING PARTY FAILED TO SUSTAIN BURDEN AND SHOW THAT PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES AND CAUSE OF ACTION THEREIN ARISE OUT OF PROTECTED ACTIVITY. COMPLAINT IS BASED ON ALLEGATION OF HABITABILITY DEFECTS AND ALSO MENTIONS IN PASSING DEFENDANT "LEAVING NOTES TO SCARE AND INTIMIDATE." COMPLAINT DOES NOT MENTION MARCH 13, 2014 NOTICE TO CURE OR QUIT AND COMPLAINT WAS SIGNED BY COUNSEL ON JULY 17, 2014, I.E. 4 DAYS BEFORE JULY 21, 2014 NOTICE TO PAY RENT OR QUIT WAS SERVED. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION. PREVAILING PARTY IS ORDERED TO PREPARE PROPOSED ORDER LEAVING THE AMOUNT OF SANCTIONS BLANK. JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. OLOPERNES, NOT REPORTED

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-08-2014 Continued To Feb-04-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-19-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Karen Y. Uchiyama, Esq., (415-563-9300), for David Levy, Defendant and moving party.
Kenneth M. Greenstein, Esq. of Greenstein & McDonald, (415-733-1240), for James Charles, Plaintiff and opposing party.

Court hears arguments re the motion and plaintiff's request for sanctions. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral arguments rules as follows: DEFENDANTS DAVID LEVY, ELEANOR LEVY, and LEVY FAMILY TRUST's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is DENIED. Moving party failed to sustain burden and show that Plaintiff's complaint for damages and cause of action therein arise out of protected activity. Complaint is based on allegation of habitability defects and also mentions in passing defendant "leaving notes to scare and intimidate." Complaint does not mention March 13, 2014 Notice to Cure or Quit and complaint was signed by counsel on July 17, 2014, i.e. 4 days before July 21, 2014 Notice to Pay Rent or Quit was served.

Plaintiff's request for sanctions is taken under submission. Prevailing party is ordered to prepare proposed order. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not Reported, D501.
